description: Critical NextGen AV data is being sent to C2C for checks; NextGen AV tooling is implemented on all critical services/applications

name: Application/Code Identification
description: Component has identified applications and classified as either legacy, virtualized on-premises, and cloud hosted

name: Data Analysis
description: The service catalog is updated with data types for each application and service based on data classification levels

description: Enforcement points are identified; Standardized Logging schema is enforced at the enterprise and org levels

name: Data Rights Management (DRM) Enforcement Point Logging and Analysis

description: Provide Technical Standards; Develop Concept of Operations; Identify Communities of Interest

name: Define SDN APIs
description: SDN APIs are standardized and implemented; APIs are functional for AuthN (Authentication) Decision Point, App Delivery Control Proxy (F5) and Segmentation Gateways

name: Policy Inventory & Development
description: Policies have been collected in reference to applicable compliance and risk (eg RMF, NIST); Policies have been reviewed for missing Pillars and Capabilities per the ZTRA; Missing areas of policies are updated to meet the capabilities per ZTRA

name: Task Automation Analysis
description: Automatable tasks are identified; Tasks are enumerated

name: Response Automation Analysis
description: Automatable response activities are identified; Response activities are enumerated

name: Tool Compliance Analysis
description: API status is determined compliance or non-compliance to API standards; Tools to be used are Identified

name: Scale Considerations
description: Sufficient infrastructure in place; Distributed environment established; Sufficient bandwidth for network traffic

name: Implement Data Tagging & Classification Tools
description: A requirement of Data classification and tagging tools must include integration and/or support of Machine Learning (ML); Data classification and tagging tools are implemented at org and enterprise levels

name: Organizational MFA/Identity Provider (IdP)
description: Component is using Identity Provider (IdP) with MFA for critical applications/services; Components have implemented an Identity Provider (Identity Provider (IdP)) that enables DoD PKI multifactor authentication; Organizational Standardized PKI for critical services

name: Implement System and Migrate Privileged Users Pt1
description: Privilege Access Management (PAM) tooling is implemented; Identified applications that support and do not support  Privilege Access Management (PAM) tools; Applications that support  Privilege Access Management (PAM), now use  Privilege Access Management (PAM) for controlling emergency/built-in accounts

name: Deny User by Default Policy
description: Applications updated to deny by default to functions/data requiring specific roles/attributes for access; Reduced default permissions levels are implemented; Applications/services have reviewed/audited all privileged users and removed those users who do not need that level of access; Applications' identify functions and data requiring specific roles/attributes for access

name: Single Authentication
description: Authentication implemented across applications per session

name: Non-person entities (NPE)/PKI, Device under Management
description: Non-person entities are managed via Org PKI and Org Identity Provider (IdP) (Identity Provider)

name: Deny Device by Default Policy
description: Components can block device access by default to resources (apps/data) and explicitly allow compliant devices per policy; Remote Access is enabled following a "deny device by default policy" approach

name: Implement Asset, Vulnerability and Patch Management Tools
description: Components can confirm if devices meet minimum compliance standards or not; Components have asset management, vulnerability, and patching systems with APIs that will enable integration across the systems

name: Implement UEDM or equivalent Tools
description: Components can confirm if devices meet minimum compliance standards or not; Components have asset management system(s) for user devices (phones, desktops, laptops) that maintains IT compliance, which is reported up to DoD enterprise; Components asset management systems can programmatically, ie, API, provide device compliance status and if it meets minimum standards

name: Enterprise Device Management Pt1
description: Manual inventory is integrated with an automated management solution for critical services; Enable ZT Device Management (from any location with or without remote access)

name: Implement Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR) Tools and Integrate with C2C
description: Endpoint Detection & Response Tooling is implemented ; Critical EDR data is being sent to C2C for checks; NextGen AV tooling covers maximum amount of services/applications

name: Build DevSecOps Software Factory Pt1
description: Developed Data/Service Standards for DevSecOps; CI/CD Pipeline is fully functional and tested successfully; Vulnerability Management program is officially in place and operating

name: Build DevSecOps Software Factory Pt2
description: Development of applications is migrated to CI/CD pipeline; Continual validation process/technology is implemented and in use; Development of applications is migrated to DevSecOps process and technology

name: Approved Binaries/Code
description: Supplier sourcing risk evaluated and identified for approved sources; Repository and update channel established for use by development teams; Bill of Materials is created for applications identify source, supportability and risk posture; Industry standard (DIB) and approved vulnerability databases are pulled in to be used in DevSecOps

name: Vulnerability Management Program Pt1
description: Vulnerability Management Team is in place w/ appropriate stakeholder membership; Vulnerability Management policy and process is in place and agreed to w/ stakeholders; Public source of vulnerabilities are being utilized for tracking

name: Resource Authorization Pt1
description: The DoD Enterprise standardizes on resource authorization approaches (e.g., Software Defined Perimeter) with the organizations. At a minimum the resource authorization gateways will be integrated with identities and devices. Organizations deploy approved resource authorization gateways and enable for external facing applications/services. Additional applications for migration and applications unable to be migrated are identified for exception or decommission

name: Software-Defined Compute (SDC) Resource Authorization Part 1 ... D-
Define Data Tagging Standards
description: Enterprise data classification and tagging standards are developed; Organizations align to enterprise standards and begin implementation

name: Interoperability Standards
description: Formal standards are in place by the Enterprise for the appropriate data standards

name: File Activity Monitoring Pt2
description: Data and files of all regulated classifications are actively being monitored; Extended integrations are in place as appropriate to further manage risk

name: Implement Data Rights Management (DRM) and Protection Tools Pt1
description: Data Rights Management (DRM) and protection tools are enabled for high risk data repositories with basic protections

name: Implement Enforcement Points

name: Define Granular Control Access Rules & Policies Pt2
description: Define Data Tagging Filters for API Infrastructure

name: Implement SDN Programable Infrastructure

description: Log Actions to SIEM; Establish Proxy/Enforcement Checks of Device Attributes, Behavior, and other Data; Analyze Activities with Analytics Engine

name: Implement Microsegmentation
description: Accept Automated Policy Changes; Implement API Decision Points; Implement NGF/Micro FW/Endpoint Agent in Virtual Hosting Environment

name: Organization Access Profile
description: Organization scoped profile(s) are created to determine access to Data, Applications, Assets, Services (DAAS)  using capabilities from User, Data, Network, and Device pillars; Initial enterprise profile access standard is developed for access to Data, Applications, Assets, Services (DAAS)  ; When possible the organization profile(s) utilizes enterprise available services in the User, Data, Network and Device pillars; Organization Mission/Task critical profile(s) are created

name: Implement SOAR Tools
description: Develop requirements for SOAR tool; Procure SOAR tools

name: Standardized API Calls & Schemas Pt1
description: Initial calls and schemas are implemented; Non-compliant tools are replaced

name: Workflow Enrichment Pt1
description: Threat events are identified; Workflows for threat events are developed

name: Log Parsing
description: Standardized log formats; Rules developed for each log format

name: Asset ID & Alert Correlation
description: Rules developed for asset ID based responses

name: Implement Analytics Tools
description: Develop requirements for analytic environment; Procure and implement analytic tools

name: Cyber Threat Intelligence Program Pt1
description: Cyber Threat Intelligence team is in place with critical stakeholders; Public and Baseline CTI feeds are being utilized by SIEM for alerting; Basic integration points exist with Device and Network enforcement points (e.g., NGAV, NGFW, NG-IPS)

name: Manual Data Tagging Pt1
description: Manual data tagging begins at the enterprise level with basic attributes

name: Implement App Based Permissions per Enterprise
description: Enterprise roles/attributes needed for user authorization to application functions and/or data have been registered with enterprise ICAM; DoD Enterprise ICAM has self-service attribute/role registration service that enables application owners to add attributes or use existing enterprise attributes; Privileged activities are fully migrated to  Privilege Access Management (PAM)

name: Rule Based Dynamic Access Pt1
description: Access to application's/service's functions and/or data are limited to users with appropriate enterprise attributes; All possible applications use JIT/JEA permissions for administrative users

name: Implement System and Migrate Privileged Users Pt2
description: Privileged activities are migrated to  Privilege Access Management (PAM) and access is fully managed

name: Enterprise Identity Life-Cycle Management Pt1
description: Automated Identity Lifecycle Processes; Integrated with Enterprise ICAM process and tools

name: Implement User & Entity Behavior Activity (User & entity behavior analysis (UEBA)) Tooling
description: User & entity behavior analysis (UEBA) functionality is implemented for Enterprise Identity Provider (IdP)

name: Periodic Authentication
description: Authentication implemented multiple times per session based on security attributes

name: Enterprise PKI/Identity Provider (IdP) Pt1
description: Components are using Identity Provider (IdP) with MFA for all applications/services; Organizational MFA/PKI integrated with Enterprise MFA/PKI; Organizational Standardized PKI for all services

name: Enterprise Identity Provider (IdP) Pt1
description: Non-person entities (NPE)s (service accounts?) including devices are integrated with Enterprise Identity Provider (IdP) (Identity Provider)

name: Implement C2C/Compliance Based Network Authorization Pt1
description: C2C Rollout starts at the enterprise level for low risk and testing environments; Basic devices checks are implemented using C2C

name: Implement Application Control & File Integrity Monitoring (FIM) Tools
description: AppControl and FIM tooling is implemented on all critical services/applications; EDR tooling covers maximum amount of services/applications; AppControl and FIM data is sent to C2C as needed

name: Managed and Limited BYOD & IOT Support
description: All applications require dynamic permissions access for devices; BYOD and IOT device permissions are baselined and integrated with Enterprise Identity Provider (IdP)

name: Enterprise Device Management Pt2
description: Manual inventory is integrated with an automated management solution for all services

name: Implement Extended Detection & Response (XDR) Tools and Integrate with C2C Pt1
description: Integration Points have been identified per Capability; Riskiest integration points have been integrated w/ XDR; Basic alerting is in place with SIEM and/or other mechanisms

name: Automate Application Security & Code Remediation Pt1
description: Secure API Gateway is operational and majority of API calls are passing through gateway; Application Security functions (e.g., code review, container and serverless security) are implemented as part of CI/CD and DevSecOps

name: Vulnerability Management Program Pt2
description: Controlled (e.g., DIB, CERT) sources of vulnerabilities are being utilized for tracking; Vulnerability management program has a process for accepting external/public disclosures for managed services

name: Continual Validation
description: Updated Applications are deployed in a live and/or production environment; Applications that were marked for retirement and transition are decommissioned; Continual validation tools are implemented and applied to code in the CI/CD pipeline; Code requiring continuous validation is identified and validation criteria are established

name: Resource Authorization Pt2
description: Resource authorization gateways are used for all possible applications/services. Application unable to utilize gateways are either decommissioned or excepted using a risk based methodical approach. Authorizations are further integrated with the CI/CD pipeline for automated decision making.

name: Protect Data In Transit

description: All calls and schemas are implemented

description: Develop analytics to detect changing threat conditions; Identify user and device threat profiles

description: Cyber Threat Intelligence team is in place with extended stakeholders as appropriate; Controlled and Private feed are being utilized by SIEM and other appropriate Analytics tools for alerting and monitoring; Integration is in place for extended enforcement points within the Device, User, Network and Data pillars (User & entity behavior analysis (UEBA), UAM)

description: Transaction authentication implemented per session based on security attributes

description: All Organizational Services Integrate w/ Biometrics

name: Enterprise Identity Provider (IdP) Pt2
description: Conditional device attributes are part of the Identity Provider (IdP) profile

description: C2C is rolled out to all supported environments; Advanced devices checks are completed and integrated with dynamic access (Enterprise Identity Provider (IdP) / ZTNA)

name: Entity Activity Monitoring Pt1
description: User & entity behavior analysis (UEBA) (User and Entity Behavior Analatyics) attributes are integrated for device baselining; User & entity behavior analysis (UEBA) attributes are available for usage with device access

name: Entity Activity Monitoring Pt2
description: User & entity behavior analysis (UEBA) attributes are mandated for device access

name: Fully Integrate Device Security stack with C2C as appropriate
description: AppControl and FIM deployment is expanded to all necessary services/applications; Remaining data from Device Security tooling is implemented with C2C

name: Enterprise PKI Pt1
description: Devices that are unable to have certificates are phased out and/or moved to minimal access environments; All devices and Non-person entities (NPE)s have certs installed for authentication in the Enterprise PKI

name: Enterprise PKI Pt2
description: Devices are required to authenticate to communicate with other services and devices

name: Managed and Full BYOD & IOT Support Pt1
description: Only BYOD and IOT devices that meet mandated configuration standards allowed to access resources; Critical Services require dynamic access for devices

name: Managed and Full BYOD & IOT Support Pt2

description: Remaining integration points have been integrate as appropriate; Extended alerting and response is enabled with other Analytics tools at least using SIEM

name: Automate Application Security & Code Remediation Pt2
description: Services are provided following a Service Oriented Architecture (SOA); Security Remediation activities (e.g., runtime security, library updates, release approvals) are fully automated

name: Enrich Attributes for Resource Authorization Pt1
description: Initial attributes from sources such as User and Entity Activity Monitoring, Micro-segmentation services, DLP and DRM are integrated into the Resource Authorization technology stack and policy. Any additional attributes for later integration are identified and planned. Attributes are used to create basic risk posture of users, NPEs and devices allowing for authorization decisions.

name: Enrich Attributes for Resource Authorization Pt2
description: Extended identified attributes are integrated with the resource authorization technology and policy. Confidence scoring is introduced across the attributes to create a more advanced method of authorization decision making in an automated fashion.

name: REST API Micro-Segments
description: Using the DoD Enterprise approved API gateway(s), application calls are micro-segmented only allowing authenticated and authorized access to specific destinations (e.g., microservices). When possible, API Micro-Segmentation consoles are integrated and aware of other Micro-Segmentation consoles such as Software Defined Perimeter Controllers and/or Software Defined Networking Consoles.

name: Continuous Authorization to Operate (cATO) Pt1
description: Controls derivation is standardized and ready for automation; Controls testing is integrated with DevSecOps processes and technology

name: Continuous Authorization to Operate (cATO) Pt2
description: Controls testing is fully automated; Integration with standard IR and SOC operations is automated; Control derivation and applicability is fully automated; Dashboards are used to track continuing authorization status

name: Automated Data Tagging & Support Pt1
